component.name: LinuxPop
component.summary: A floating popup of context-aware actions for selected text

description.p: Select some text and LinuxPop pops up a little bar of buttons that fit what you grabbed: search it, copy it, open links and paths, send it to an AI assistant, encode or decode it, translate it, run it as a quick script. It's the PopClip idea from macOS, done for Linux. Works on X11 and on KDE Plasma 6 / Wayland.

ul.li: Floating action popup that appears at your text selection
Runs on both X11 and KDE Plasma 6 / Wayland
Context-aware buttons (URL decode only shows on encoded URLs, etc.)
Send any selection to ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Perplexity or local Ollama
In-place translation bubble with a language picker
Clipboard manager + snippets picker on a configurable hotkey
No-code recipe wizard for building your own custom buttons
Plugin marketplace with one-click install of community actions

screenshot.caption: The action popup near a text selection
Settings with the premium dark theme

description.p: The editing buttons work in the Flatpak again. Select all, Cut, Paste, Backspace and paste-and-send all press real keys, and on KDE Wayland that needs a tool the sandbox does not carry, so they had been doing nothing. They now reach the host's key-injection tool the same way the terminal and file actions do, so the buttons behave like a normal install.

Screen OCR feels like it used to again. The Flatpak build had been routing capture through the desktop's screenshot portal, which on KDE popped Spectacle's full window and an extra confirm step. It now freezes the screen and lets you drag one rectangle, the same quick flow as a normal install, by driving the host's own capture tool. Recognition still runs on the bundled engine, no setup needed.

Your settings finally stick. In the Flatpak build the app was saving its settings, custom buttons, snippets and clipboard history to a temporary folder that the sandbox wiped on every restart, so things like switching off a search button never held. It now writes to its own permanent folder, so whatever you change stays changed.

Flatpak: screen OCR is back (bundled tesseract plus the Screenshot portal); run-in-terminal and run-command now run on the host; open file/folder works for any path; dropped the home filesystem grant that was blocking the app from saving its own settings.

Flatpak: the popup's cursor/selection positioning now works fully inside the sandbox without host /tmp access. The KWin helper script is written to the app's runtime dir instead. Nothing changes outside Flatpak.

ul.li: Runs on X11 and on KDE Plasma 6 / Wayland
Built-in plugins + a JSON-defined recipe system
In-place translation bubble with a language picker

description.p: Note: this beta focused on KDE Plasma 6 / Wayland. X11 desktops (including Linux Mint Cinnamon) work but aren't fully re-verified in 0.9.0 yet. Bug reports are very welcome.
